---
title: "Problem Set 2: Change, Communication, And Visualization Choice"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

# Due Date

Due Tuesday, June 16, 2026, at 11:59 pm.

# Purpose

This problem set asks you to make and justify visualizations for change over time and communication.

The focus is on the second half of the course:

- change over time
- grouping and small multiples
- uncertainty or intervals when available
- choosing a visualization from the analytic task
- improving a rough plot into a clearer final figure
- written explanation of design choices

# Dataset

Use the cleaned CDC flu vaccination dataset:

```text
data/real/flu_vaccination_age_time_clean.csv
```

Relevant codebook:

```text
data/codebooks/flu_vaccination_age_time_clean.md
```

# Submission

Submit a rendered HTML notebook.

Your notebook should include code, output, visualizations, and written interpretation.

# Required Tasks

## Task 1: Dataset Orientation

Describe:

- what one row represents
- the time variable or variables
- the grouping variable or variables you plan to use
- one limitation or caution about the data

## Task 2: Change Over Time

Create one visualization that shows change over time.

Possible directions:

- vaccination estimate over season start year
- trends by age group
- trends for selected months
- changes in national or regional estimates over time

Explain why the visualization form is appropriate for change.

## Task 3: Grouping Or Small Multiples

Create one visualization that shows how the time pattern differs across groups.

Possible directions:

- facet by age group
- color by age group
- compare selected seasons
- compare selected months

Explain what grouping adds to the analysis.

## Task 4: Uncertainty Or Data Quality

Use the confidence interval columns or sample size column in one visualization or written diagnostic.

You do not need a complicated uncertainty plot. The goal is to show that you noticed the uncertainty or data-quality information and used it responsibly.

## Task 5: Rough Plot To Final Plot

Choose one plot from this problem set and show how you improved it.

Include:

- a rough first version
- a revised final version
- a short explanation of what changed and why

# Expectations

Your submission should include at least three visualizations.

At least one visualization should show change over time.

At least one visualization should compare groups.

At least one visualization should include either confidence interval information, sample size information, or a written caution based on those fields.

# Rubric

Problem Set 2 is worth 30 points.

| Section | Points |
|---|---:|
| Task 1: Dataset Orientation | 5 |
| Task 2: Change Over Time | 5 |
| Task 3: Grouping Or Small Multiples | 5 |
| Task 4: Uncertainty Or Data Quality | 5 |
| Task 5: Rough Plot To Final Plot | 5 |
| Presentation And Clarity | 5 |

The same criteria will be applied to each 5-point section:

| Score | Meaning |
|---:|---|
| 5 | Complete, thorough, and correct. |
| 4 | Complete and mostly correct, with minor gaps in explanation, polish, or interpretation. |
| 3 | Partially complete, with important gaps in completeness, thoroughness, or correctness. |
| 2 | Minimal attempt, with major issues in completeness or correctness. |
| 0-1 | Missing, not assessable, or not responsive to the task. |
